31: 32: 33: 34: 35: --- 36: 37: Chatwoot is the modern, open-source, and self-hosted customer support platform designed to help businesses deliver exceptional customer support experience. Built for scale and flexibility, Chatwoot gives you full control over your customer data while providing powerful tools to manage conversations across channels. 38: 39: ### ✨ Captain – AI Agent for Support 40: 41: Supercharge your support with Captain, Chatwoot’s AI agent. Captain helps automate responses, handle common queries, and reduce agent workload—ensuring customers get instant, accurate answers. With Captain, your team can focus on complex conversations while routine questions are resolved automatically. Read more about Captain [here](https://chwt.app/captain-docs). 42: 43: ### 💬 Omnichannel Support Desk 44: 45: Chatwoot centralizes all customer conversations into one powerful inbox, no matter where your customers reach out from. It supports live chat on your website, email, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, WhatsApp, Telegram, Line, SMS etc. 46: 47: ### 📚 Help center portal 48: 49: Publish help articles, FAQs, and guides through the built-in Help Center Portal. Enable customers to find answers on their own, reduce repetitive queries, and keep your support team focused on more complex issues. 50: 51: ### 🗂️ Other features 52: 53: #### Collaboration & Productivity 54: 55: - Private Notes and @mentions for internal team discussions. 56: - Labels to organize and categorize conversations. 57: - Keyboard Shortcuts and a Command Bar for quick navigation. 58: - Canned Responses to reply faster to frequently asked questions. 59: - Auto-Assignment to route conversations based on agent availability. 60: - Multi-lingual Support to serve customers in multiple languages. 61: - Custom Views and Filters for better inbox organization. 62: - Business Hours and Auto-Responders to manage response expectations. 63: - Teams and Automation tools for scaling support workflows. 64: - Agent Capacity Management to balance workload across the team. 65: 66: #### Customer Data & Segmentation 67: - Contact Management with profiles and interaction history. 68: - Contact Segments and Notes for targeted communication. 69: - Campaigns to proactively engage customers. 70: - Custom Attributes for storing additional customer data. 71: - Pre-Chat Forms to collect user information before starting conversations. 72: 73: #### Integrations 74: - Slack Integration to manage conversations directly from Slack. 75: - Dialogflow Integration for chatbot automation. 76: - Dashboard Apps to embed internal tools within Chatwoot. 77: - Shopify Integration to view and manage customer orders right within Chatwoot. 78: - Use Google Translate to translate messages from your customers in realtime. 79: - Create and manage Linear tickets within Chatwoot. 80: 81: #### Reports & Insights 82: - Live View of ongoing conversations for real-time monitoring. 83: - Conversation, Agent, Inbox, Label, and Team Reports for operational visibility. 84: - CSAT Reports to measure customer satisfaction. 85: - Downloadable Reports for offline analysis and reporting. 86: 87: 88: ## Documentation 89: 90: Detailed documentation is available at [chatwoot.com/help-center](https://www.chatwoot.com/help-center). 91: 92: ## Translation process 93: 94: The translation process for Chatwoot web and mobile app is managed at [https://translate.chatwoot.com](https://translate.chatwoot.com) using Crowdin. Please read the [translation guide](https://www.chatwoot.com/docs/contributing/translating-chatwoot-to-your-language) for contributing to Chatwoot. 95: 96: ## Branching model 97: 98: We use the [git-flow](https://nvie.com/posts/a-successful-git-branching-model/) branching model. The base branch is `develop`. 99: If you are looking for a stable version, please use the `master` or tags labelled as `v1.x.x`. 100: 101: ## Deployment 102: 103: ### Heroku one-click deploy 104: 105: Deploying Chatwoot to Heroku is a breeze. It's as simple as clicking this button: 106: 107: [![Deploy](https://www.herokucdn.com/deploy/button.svg)](https://heroku.com/deploy?template=https://github.com/chatwoot/chatwoot/tree/master) 108: 109: Follow this [link](https://www.chatwoot.com/docs/environment-variables) to understand setting the correct environment variables for the app to work with all the features. There might be breakages if you do not set the relevant environment variables. 110: 111: 112: ### DigitalOcean 1-Click Kubernetes deployment 113: 114: Chatwoot now supports 1-Click deployment to DigitalOcean as a kubernetes app. 115: 116: 117: 118: 119: 120: ### Other deployment options 121: 122: For other supported options, checkout our [deployment page](https://chatwoot.com/deploy). 123: 124: ## Security 125: 126: Looking to report a vulnerability? Please refer our [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) file. 127: 128: ## Community 129: 130: If you need help or just want to hang out, come, say hi on our [Discord](https://discord.gg/cJXdrwS) server. 131: 132: ## Contributors 133: 134: Thanks goes to all these [wonderful people](https://www.chatwoot.com/docs/contributors): 135: 136: 137: 138: 139: *Chatwoot* © 2017-2026, Chatwoot Inc - Released under the MIT License. ## Inbox Destruction Flow - Complete Analysis

Based on my analysis of the Chatwoot codebase, here's the complete picture of what happens when an Inbox is destroyed:

---

### **1. Destruction Trigger (Controller Layer)**

**File: `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:77-80`**
- `destroy` action calls `::DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip)`
- This is an **asynchronous** job-based destruction

---

### **2. Core Destruction Job**

**File: `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:6-12`**
- `DeleteObjectJob#perform` is the entry point for Inbox destruction
- It **first** purges heavy associations (line 9), **then** calls `object.destroy!` (line 10)

**File: `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:18-23`**
- `heavy_associations` method defines what gets pre-purged for Inbox:
  - `conversations`
  - `contact_inboxes`
  - `reporting_events`

**File: `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:36-40`**
- `batch_destroy` processes records in batches of 5,000

---

### **3. Inbox Model Callbacks & Associations**

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:60`**
- `belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy` — **inline destruction**

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:62-76`** — All use `dependent: :destroy_async`:
- `campaigns` (line 62)
- `contact_inboxes` (line 63)
- `inbox_members` (line 66)
- `conversations` (line 68)
- `messages` (line 69)
- `agent_bot_inbox` (line 73)
- `webhooks` (line 75)
- `hooks` (Integrations::Hook) (line 76)

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:71`**
- `has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy` — **inline destruction**

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:80`**
- `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` — **custom callback after destruction**

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:260-262`**
- `delete_round_robin_agents` calls `::AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService.new(inbox: self).clear_queue`
- This clears Redis keys (does not delete data, just removes queue state)

---

### **4. Associated Record Cleanup**

#### **Inline Destruction** (happens during `destroy!`):
- `channel` (line 60 in inbox.rb:60)
- `inbox_assignment_policy` (line 71 in inbox.rb:71)
- `inbox_members` via `InboxMember` model's association to `user` and `inbox`

#### **Async Destruction** (via `destroy_async`, processed by `DeleteObjectJob`):
- `conversations` — also triggers its own callbacks (see below)
- `contact_inboxes` — also triggers its own cleanup
- `messages` — also triggers its own cleanup
- `campaigns`
- `webhooks`
- `hooks` (Integrations::Hook)
- `agent_bot_inbox`
- `reporting_events` (via Reportable concern, `dependent: :destroy` — **inline**)

---

### **5. Cascade Effects on Associated Models**

#### **Conversation** (`app/models/conversation.rb:109-116`):
When conversations are destroyed (as part of inbox cleanup):
- `mentions` — `dependent: :destroy_async`
- `messages` — `dependent: :destroy_async`
- `csat_survey_response` — `dependent: :destroy_async`
- `conversation_participants` — `dependent: :destroy_async`
- `notifications` — `dependent: :destroy_async`
- `reporting_events` — `dependent: :destroy_async`

**Conversation callbacks** (`app/models/conversation.rb:124-125`):
- `before_destroy :set_unread_count_deletion_data` (line 124)
- `after_destroy_commit :notify_conversation_deletion` (line 125)

#### **ContactInbox** (`app/models/contact_inbox.rb:34`):
When contact_inboxes are destroyed (as part of inbox cleanup):
- `conversations` — `dependent: :destroy_async`

#### **Message** (`app/models/message.rb:133-135`):
When messages are destroyed (as part of inbox cleanup):
- `attachments` — `dependent: :destroy` (**inline**, line 133)
- `csat_survey_response` — `dependent: :destroy_async` (line 134)
- `notifications` — `dependent: :destroy_async` (line 135)

#### **InboxMember** (`app/models/inbox_member.rb:26`):
- `after_destroy :remove_agent_from_round_robin` (line 26)
- Calls `InboxRoundRobinService.new(inbox: inbox).remove_agent_from_queue(user_id)`
- **Only if inbox is present** (line 35 in inbox_member.rb:35)

#### **AgentBotInbox** (`app/models/agent_bot_inbox.rb:14-21`):
- Belongs to inbox and agent_bot
- No explicit destroy callbacks

#### **ReportingEvent** (`app/models/reporting_event.rb:30-38`):
- Belongs to inbox (optional), account, conversation, user
- No destroy callbacks
- Cleanup via `dependent: :destroy` in Reportable concern (`app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:7`)

#### **Webhook** (`app/models/webhook.rb:21-23`):
- Belongs to inbox (optional) and account
- No destroy callbacks

#### **Integrations::Hook** (`app/models/integrations/hook.rb:17-43`):
- Belongs to inbox (optional) and account
- No destroy callbacks

---

### **6. Custom Destroy Logic**

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:260-262`**
- `delete_round_robin_agents` clears Redis round-robin queue
- Executes **after** inbox is destroyed

**File: `app/models/inbox_member.rb:34-36`**
- `remove_agent_from_round_robin` only runs if `inbox.present?`
- This means it won't run when inbox is being destroyed (inbox will be nil in after_destroy)

**File: `app/models/inbox.rb:260`**
- Note: `InboxMember.after_destroy` runs **before** `Inbox.after_destroy` because:
  - InboxMember has foreign key to Inbox
  - When Inbox is destroyed, InboxMember records are deleted first (due to `dependent: :destroy_async`)
  - But since the inbox record is being destroyed, `inbox.present?` check in line 35 may fail

---

### **7. Order of Operations During Inbox Destruction**

1. **`DeleteObjectJob#perform` is enqueued** (from controller `destroy` action)
2. **`DeleteObjectJob#purge_heavy_associations` runs**:
   - Batches destroy `reporting_events` (via `batch_destroy`)
   - Batches destroy `contact_inboxes` (via `batch_destroy`)
   - Batches destroy `conversations` (via `batch_destroy`)
3. **Inbox#destroy! is called** (line 10 in delete_object_job.rb:10)
4. **Direct `dependent: :destroy` associations clean up**:
   - `channel` destroyed inline
   - `inbox_assignment_policy` destroyed inline
5. **`destroy_async` associations trigger their own jobs**:
   - `campaigns` (async)
   - `inbox_members` (async)
   - `messages` (async)
   - `webhooks` (async)
   - `hooks` (async)
   - `agent_bot_inbox` (async)
6. **`after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` callback runs** (inbox.rb:80)
7. **InboxMember `after_destroy` callbacks run** (as InboxMember records are destroyed):
   - `remove_agent_from_round_robin` (but inbox may be nil at this point)

---

### **8. Async vs Inline Summary**

**Async (via `destroy_async`, processed in batches by DeleteObjectJob):**
- conversations (app/models/inbox.rb:68)
- contact_inboxes (app/models/inbox.rb:63)
- messages (app/models/inbox.rb:69)
- campaigns (app/models/inbox.rb:62)
- inbox_members (app/models/inbox.rb:66)
- webhooks (app/models/inbox.rb:75)
- hooks (app/models/inbox.rb:76)
- agent_bot_inbox (app/models/inbox.rb:73)
- reporting_events (for Account only, not Inbox — app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:21)

**Inline (via `dependent: :destroy`, immediate destruction):**
- channel (app/models/inbox.rb:60)
- inbox_assignment_policy (app/models/inbox.rb:71)
- inbox_members' user associations (app/models/inbox_member.rb:23-24)
- reporting_events (via Reportable concern — app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:7)
- attachments on Message (app/models/message.rb:133)

**Note:** `destroy_async` means the association cleanup happens via a **separate job**, not that it happens asynchronously in the background per se. The association still uses `destroy!` but is triggered via a job queue.

---

### **9. Key Callbacks Summary**

- **`after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents`** (app/models/inbox.rb:80)
  - Calls: `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#clear_queue`
  - Deletes Redis key: `round_robin_key`
  - No data destruction, only Redis cleanup

- **`InboxMember#after_destroy :remove_agent_from_round_robin`** (app/models/inbox_member.rb:26)
  - Calls: `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#remove_agent_from_queue`
  - Only if inbox.present? (may fail during inbox destruction)

---

### **10. Enterprise Edition Overlays**

**File: `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb:1-41`**
- Extends member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
- No destroy-related extensions found
- No additional callbacks or cleanup logic

---

7:     # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid

9:     purge_heavy_associations(object)
10:     object.destroy!

23:   end

26:     klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
27:     return unless klass

30:       next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)

38:       batch.each(&:destroy!)

1: class Captain::InboxPendingConversationsResolutionJob < ApplicationJob
2:   CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_RESOLVE_ACTIVITY_REASON = 'no outstanding questions'.freeze
3:   CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_HANDOFF_ACTIVITY_REASON = 'pending clarification from customer'.freeze
4: 
5:   queue_as :low
6: 
7:   def perform(inbox)
8:     return if inbox.account.captain_auto_resolve_disabled?
9: 
10:     if evaluate_conversation_completion?(inbox.account)
11:       perform_with_evaluation(inbox)
12:     else
13:       perform_time_based(inbox)
14:     end
15:   ensure
16:     Current.reset
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def evaluate_conversation_completion?(account)
22:     account.feature_enabled?('captain_tasks') && account.captain_auto_resolve_evaluated?
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform_time_based(inbox)
26:     Current.executed_by = inbox.captain_assistant
27: 
28:     resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox).each do |conversation|
29:       create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
30:       conversation.resolved!
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   def perform_with_evaluation(inbox)
35:     Current.executed_by = inbox.captain_assistant
36: 
37:     resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox).each do |conversation|
38:       evaluation = evaluate_conversation(conversation, inbox)
39:       next unless still_resolvable_after_evaluation?(conversation)
40: 
41:       if evaluation[:complete]
42:         resolve_conversation(conversation, inbox, evaluation[:reason])
43:       else
44:         handoff_conversation(conversation, inbox, evaluation[:reason])
45:       end
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def evaluate_conversation(conversation, inbox)
50:     Captain::ConversationCompletionService.new(
51:       account: inbox.account,
52:       conversation_display_id: conversation.display_id
53:     ).perform
54:   end
55: 
56:   def resolvable_pending_conversations(inbox)
57:     inbox.conversations.pending
58:          .where('last_activity_at < ?', auto_resolve_cutoff_time)
59:          .limit(Limits::BULK_ACTIONS_LIMIT)
60:   end
61: 
62:   def still_resolvable_after_evaluation?(conversation)
63:     conversation.reload
64:     conversation.pending? && conversation.last_activity_at < auto_resolve_cutoff_time
65:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
66:     false
67:   end
68: 
69:   def auto_resolve_cutoff_time
70:     Time.now.utc - 1.hour
71:   end
72: 
73:   def resolve_conversation(conversation, inbox, reason)
74:     create_private_note(conversation, inbox, "Auto-resolved: #{reason}")
75:     create_resolution_message(conversation, inbox)
76:     conversation.with_captain_activity_context(
77:       reason: CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_RESOLVE_ACTIVITY_REASON,
78:       reason_type: :inference
79:     ) { conversation.resolved! }
80:     conversation.dispatch_captain_inference_resolved_event
81:   end
82: 
83:   def handoff_conversation(conversation, inbox, reason)
84:     create_private_note(conversation, inbox, "Auto-handoff: #{reason}")
85:     create_handoff_message(conversation, inbox)
86:     conversation.with_captain_activity_context(
87:       reason: CAPTAIN_INFERENCE_HANDOFF_ACTIVITY_REASON,
88:       reason_type: :inference
89:     ) { conversation.bot_handoff! }
90:     conversation.dispatch_captain_inference_handoff_event
91:     send_out_of_office_message_if_applicable(conversation.reload)
92:   end
93: 
94:   def send_out_of_office_message_if_applicable(conversation)
95:     # Campaign conversations should never receive OOO templates — the campaign itself
96:     # serves as the initial outreach, and OOO would be confusing in that context.
97:     return if conversation.campaign.present?
98: 
99:     ::MessageTemplates::Template::OutOfOffice.perform_if_applicable(conversation)
100:   end

103:     conversation.messages.create!(

105:       private: true,
106:       sender: inbox.captain_assistant,
107:       account_id: conversation.account_id,
108:       inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
109:       content: content
110:     )
111:   end

115:       resolution_message = inbox.captain_assistant.config['resolution_message']
116:       conversation.messages.create!(
117:         message_type: :outgoing,
118:         account_id: conversation.account_id,
119:         inbox_id: conversation.inbox_id,
120:         content: resolution_message.presence || I18n.t('conversations.activity.auto_resolution_message'),
121:         sender: inbox.captain_assistant

123:     end
124:   end

1: class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
5: 
6:   # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
7:   # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
8:   # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
9:   def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
10:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
11:     token = SecureRandom.uuid
12:     return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
13: 
14:     return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
15: 
16:     # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
17:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
18:     false
19:   rescue StandardError
20:     # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
21:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
22:     raise
23:   end
24: 
25:   def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
26:     inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
27:     return unless inbox
28: 
29:     service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
30:     assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
31:     Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
32:   rescue StandardError => e
33:     Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
34:     raise e if Rails.env.test?
35:   ensure
36:     release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
37:   end
38: 
39:   private
40: 
41:   # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
42:   # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
43:   # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
44:   def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
45:     return if token.nil?
46: 
47:     key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
48:     ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
49:   end
50: 
51:   def bulk_assignment_limit
52:     ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
53:   end
54: end
